name: Double Chocolate, Chocolate Chip Sandwich Cookies
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: NATHALIE DUPREE COOKS SHOW#ND7129
Preheat oven to 375°F. In a small pan over low heat, melt the butter and chocolate together. Meanwhile, mix the flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt together. In another bowl, beat the brown sugar, granulated sugar, vanilla, sour cream or buttermilk, and beaten egg. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet mixture, stirring until just combined. Stir in the chopped nuts and chocolate chips.
Grease cookie sheets with butter. For 3-inch round cookies, drop rounded teaspoonfuls of batter onto the sheets, leaving about 2 inches between each cookie. Bake for 8-10 minutes or until lightly golden. For 4-inch round cookies, drop rounded tablespoonfuls of batter onto the sheets, leaving about 2 inches between each cookie. Bake for 12-15 minutes.
Remove from oven and let cool on wire racks. To make ganache filling, melt chocolate and butter in a small pan over low heat or microwave. Gradually whisk in cream until smooth. Let stand at room temperature, stirring occasionally, for 30 minutes or longer, until barely thickened. If needed, refrigerate the mixture briefly to speed up setting.
Turn half of the cooled cookies upside down on wax paper. Spread ganache on each cookie and quickly cover with remaining cookies right side up, pressing gently to spread filling evenly. Let cool completely before serving. Makes 20 small or 9 large sandwiches.
